text: The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position with buttocks taped aside. Retractors are placed to expose the surgical field. A probe is inserted through the internal opening to explore the high intersphincteric fistula tract, revealing several milliliters of purulent discharge. A swab is taken for microbiological analysis.

The surgeon performs a phthalotomy using monopolar electrocautery with a probe placed from the internal opening to the end of the blind tract. The open fistula tract is then curetted, followed by marsupialization.
